1. Label TEE-capable nodes with the Node: 1. Label TEE-capable nodes with the Node Feature Discovery operator.
2. Enable confidential containers and cr: 2. Enable confidential containers and create a KataConfig to install the kata-cc runtime.
3. Build initdata and deploy a pod with: 3. Build initdata and deploy a pod with runtimeClassName: kata-cc, then verify attestation.
A 200 response (for example \"success\"): A 200 response (for example "success") confirms attestation. Connection refused or a policy error means attestation has not completed — check the Trustee logs and the RVPS reference values (PCR8).
A name for this Trustee deployment (the: A name for this Trustee deployment (the TrusteeConfig resource).
Allow oc/kubectl exec into the confident: Allow oc/kubectl exec into the confidential VM (recommended off).
Allow reading container stdout/stderr st: Allow reading container stdout/stderr streams (recommended off).
Allow replacing the Kata Agent policy at: Allow replacing the Kata Agent policy at runtime (recommended off).
Allow the guest to pull container images: Allow the guest to pull container images.
Allow writing to container stdin (recomm: Allow writing to container stdin (recommended off).
Applying this MachineConfig rolls out a: Applying this MachineConfig rolls out a reboot to every node in the "{{role}}" pool, one at a time.
Attestation happens inside the confident: Attestation happens inside the confidential VM, so the definitive check runs from a terminal. Fetch a resource from the Confidential Data Hub — a successful response means the TEE was attested by Trustee and the secret was released:
Attestation is provided by the Red Hat b: Attestation is provided by the Red Hat build of Trustee, which verifies TEE evidence and releases sealed secrets. It commonly runs on a separate trusted cluster (hub-and-spoke), so it may not appear here.
Attestation rules for confidential GPU e: Attestation rules for confidential GPU evidence.
Authorizes which secrets an attested cli: Authorizes which secrets an attested client may retrieve after attestation.
Available once the kata-cc runtime is in: Available once the kata-cc runtime is installed on your TEE nodes.
Confidential containers are enabled, so: Confidential containers are enabled, so the operator installs the kata-cc runtime.
Confidential containers run your workloa: Confidential containers run your workload inside a hardware Trusted Execution Environment (Intel TDX or AMD SEV-SNP), so data stays encrypted in use — even from the cluster host. Work through the checklist below to go from a fresh cluster to an attested confidential workload.
Confidential containers run your workload inside a hardware Trusted Execution Environment (Intel TDX or AMD SEV-SNP), so data stays encrypted in use — even from the host. To get started:
Confidential workloads use a kata-cc run: Confidential workloads use a kata-cc runtime class and run inside a hardware TEE. Deploy one with runtimeClassName: kata-cc and initdata to see it here.
Could not enable Intel TDX host: Could not enable Intel TDX host
Create a KataConfig with confidential co: Create a KataConfig with confidential containers enabled to install the kata-cc runtime on your TEE nodes. This reboots the selected nodes.
Delete {{kind}} {{name}} in {{namespace}: Delete {{kind}} {{name}} in {{namespace}}? Its confidential VM will be torn down.
Deploy a workload with runtimeClassName:: Deploy a workload with runtimeClassName: kata-cc, then verify its attestation from the workload page.
Deploy the Red Hat build of Trustee to v: Deploy the Red Hat build of Trustee to verify TEE evidence and release sealed secrets. It can also run on a separate trusted cluster (hub-and-spoke).
Detect and label your Intel TDX or AMD S: Detect and label your Intel TDX or AMD SEV-SNP nodes with Node Feature Discovery. If a node has TDX enabled in firmware but is not detected, activate the TDX host kernel arguments first. Confidential workloads only schedule onto these nodes.
Enable confidential containers in the Op: Enable confidential containers in the OpenShift sandboxed containers operator — a supported configuration option. The operator then installs the kata-cc runtime on your TEE nodes.
Expected measurement values the Referenc: Expected measurement values the Reference Value Provider Service checks against TEE evidence — including the PCR8 hash produced by the initdata builder.
Fill in the form and select Generate. Th: Fill in the form and select Generate. The initdata is built in your browser — nothing is sent anywhere.
How the Key Broker Service is reachable. ClusterIP keeps it in-cluster; use NodePort or LoadBalancer to reach it from a separate (hub-and-spoke) cluster.
HTTP response headers indicate success. A failure usually means an egress firewall is blocking nras.attestation.nvidia.com.
In remote mode, Trustee must reach NVIDI: In remote mode, Trustee must reach NVIDIA NRAS over egress HTTPS. Confirm connectivity from the Trustee pod:
Installing the kata-cc runtime reconfigu: Installing the kata-cc runtime reconfigures and reboots the sandboxed-containers nodes, one at a time.
Intel TDX must be activated in the host: Intel TDX must be activated in the host kernel before Node Feature Discovery can detect it. This adds the required kernel arguments to the selected MachineConfigPool. Use it when a node has TDX enabled in firmware but is not detected as TEE-capable.
Name: Name
No nodes are labeled with a Trusted Exec: No nodes are labeled with a Trusted Execution Environment yet. Use Enable TEE detection to set up Node Feature Discovery and label Intel TDX or AMD SEV-SNP nodes.
No results match the current filters: No results match the current filters
No secret resources are configured for d: No secret resources are configured for delivery yet. Add them to the generated KbsConfig spec.kbsSecretResources.
No TEE-capable nodes detected. Install t: No TEE-capable nodes detected. Install the Node Feature Discovery operator and a NodeFeatureRule to label TDX / SEV-SNP nodes.
Nodes are labeled by the Node Feature Di: Nodes are labeled by the Node Feature Discovery operator via a NodeFeatureRule: intel.feature.node.kubernetes.io/tdx and amd.feature.node.kubernetes.io/snp mark TEE hardware; nvidia.com/cc.mode.state and nvidia.com/cc.ready.state mark confidential-computing GPUs.
One will be created so NFD actually scan: One will be created so NFD actually scans your nodes.
Optional TLS secret used to verify attes: Optional TLS secret used to verify attestation tokens issued by KBS.
Paste the certificate between BEGIN/END: Paste the certificate between BEGIN/END lines, or leave blank for insecure HTTP.
Paste the gzip+base64 value, or generate: Paste the gzip+base64 value, or generate it first.
PCR8 reference value (add to Trustee RVP: PCR8 reference value (add to Trustee RVPS)
Permissive accepts most attestation evid: Permissive accepts most attestation evidence — good for getting started in dev and test.
Pin the attestation policy and KBS endpo: Pin the attestation policy and KBS endpoint into your pods with an initdata document, so the guest can attest on boot.
Restricted enforces strict attestation p: Restricted enforces strict attestation policies and requires TLS — use it for production.
Rules the Attestation Service applies to: Rules the Attestation Service applies to CPU TEE evidence (Intel TDX / AMD SEV-SNP).
The ConfigMap \"{{name}}\" was not found: The ConfigMap "{{name}}" was not found in namespace "{{namespace}}". It is generated by the Trustee operator from the TrusteeConfig.
The GPU hardware model (for example, H10: The GPU hardware model (for example, H100).
The kata-cc runtime class is created whe: The kata-cc runtime class is created when confidential containers are enabled and a KataConfig is installed on TEE-capable nodes.
The Red Hat build of Trustee brokers sec: The Red Hat build of Trustee brokers secrets to confidential workloads after verifying their attestation evidence. Create a TrusteeConfig to deploy and configure it — one resource generates the KBS, attestation policies, reference values, and secrets.
The role label of the MachineConfigPool: The role label of the MachineConfigPool holding your TEE nodes — for example "worker", or a custom pool such as "kata-oc".
This enables confidential containers in: This enables confidential containers in the OpenShift sandboxed containers operator — a supported configuration option. The operator then installs the kata-cc runtime on your TEE nodes.
This is the single resource you are crea: This is the single resource you are creating. The operator reconciles everything else from it.
This labels nodes that have a Trusted Ex: This labels nodes that have a Trusted Execution Environment (Intel TDX, AMD SEV-SNP) so confidential workloads can be scheduled on them. The Node Feature Discovery operator does the scanning.
TLS secret that terminates HTTPS on the: TLS secret that terminates HTTPS on the KBS endpoint. Required for the Restricted profile.
Trustee is deployed on this cluster and: Trustee is deployed on this cluster and ready to verify TEE evidence.
Trustee is the Red Hat build of the conf: Trustee is the Red Hat build of the confidential containers attestation service. From this single resource, the operator deploys the Key Broker Service (KBS) and its attestation and resource policies, reference values, and secrets. Your confidential workloads then attest to Trustee when they boot and, if their TEE evidence is trusted, receive their sealed secrets.
Where the Key Broker Service and its sec: Where the Key Broker Service and its secrets are created. Defaults to the Trustee operator namespace.
Whether NRAS successfully verified the G: Whether NRAS successfully verified the GPU attestation evidence.
Whether the GPU has Confidential Computi: Whether the GPU has Confidential Computing enabled.
Your GPU attestation policy should valid: Your GPU attestation policy should validate the claims NRAS returns:
